title: Docker Daemon Resource Limits Monitoring
app: Splunk Connect for Docker, host metrics (Telegraf/OTel)
spl:
index=containers sourcetype="docker:info"
| eval mem_total_gb=round(MemTotal/1073741824, 2)
| eval mem_avail_gb=round(MemAvailable/1073741824, 2)
| eval mem_used_pct=round((MemTotal-MemAvailable)/MemTotal*100, 1)
| where mem_used_pct > 85 OR NCPU < 2
| table _time host mem_total_gb mem_avail_gb mem_used_pct NCPU
| sort -mem_used_pct
description: Host-level CPU, memory, and storage pressure on the Docker engine starves containers before per-container limits trigger; early detection avoids fleet-wide slowdowns.
value: Host-level CPU, memory, and storage pressure on the Docker engine starves containers before per-container limits trigger; early detection avoids fleet-wide slowdowns.
implementation: Ingest `docker info` JSON (or `docker system df`) on an interval plus host memory/CPU from the node. Correlate with `docker:events` throttling and OOM. Alert when host memory used >85% or CPU saturation sustained >10 minutes.
visualization: Line chart (mem %, CPU load), Table (host, limits), Single value (hosts over threshold).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Connect for Docker, host metrics (Telegraf/OTel).
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=docker:info`, `sourcetype=docker:system`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest `docker info` JSON (or `docker system df`) on an interval plus host memory/CPU from the node. Correlate with `docker:events` throttling and OOM. Alert when host memory used >85% or CPU saturation sustained >10 minutes.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=containers sourcetype="docker:info"
| eval mem_total_gb=round(MemTotal/1073741824, 2)
| eval mem_avail_gb=round(MemAvailable/1073741824, 2)
| eval mem_used_pct=round((MemTotal-MemAvailable)/MemTotal*100, 1)
| where mem_used_pct > 85 OR NCPU < 2
| table _time host mem_total_gb mem_avail_gb mem_used_pct NCPU
| sort -mem_used_pct
```

Understanding this SPL

**Docker Daemon Resource Limits Monitoring** ‚Äî Host-level CPU, memory, and storage pressure on the Docker engine starves containers before per-container limits trigger; early detection avoids fleet-wide slowdowns.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=docker:info`, `sourcetype=docker:system`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Connect for Docker, host metrics (Telegraf/OTel). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: containers; **sourcetype**: docker:info. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=containers, sourcetype="docker:info". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **mem_total_gb** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **mem_avail_gb** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **mem_used_pct** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where mem_used_pct > 85 OR NCPU < 2` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **Docker Daemon Resource Limits Monitoring**): table _time host mem_total_gb mem_avail_gb mem_used_pct NCPU
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (mem %, CPU load), Table (host, limits), Single value (hosts over threshold).
grandmaExplanation: We keep an eye on docker daemon resource limits monitoring by tracking the same story your metrics tell: Host-level CPU, memory, and storage pressure on the Docker engine starves containers before per-container limits trigger; early detection avoids fleet-wide slowdowns..
knownFalsePositives: Test clusters, change windows, and blue-green or canary rollouts can trigger the same signals as incidents; tune namespaces, schedules, and thresholds to match how production is expected to behave.
